title: cyanthillium cinereum var. lanatum
intro: Cyanthillium cinereum var. lanatum, also known as the hairy black-jack, is a perennial herb native to tropical Africa. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are believed to aid in wound healing and reducing swelling. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to alleviate fever, reduce pain, and promote digestion, making it a valuable remedy for various health issues. In herbal preparations, the plant can be used as a tincture, infusion, or decoction to harness its medicinal properties, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
